      1 -#include <assert.h>
      2 -#include <mrsh/ast.h>
      3 -#include <stdbool.h>
      4 -#include <stdio.h>
      5 -#include <stdlib.h>
      6 -#include <string.h>
      7 -
      8 -#define L_LINE "│ "
      9 -#define L_VAL  "├─"
     10 -#define L_LAST "└─"
     11 -#define L_GAP  "  "
     12 -
     13 -static size_t make_sub_prefix(const char *prefix, bool last, char *buf) {
     14 -	if (buf != NULL) {
     15 -		memcpy(buf, prefix, strlen(prefix) + 1);
     16 -		strcat(buf, last ? L_GAP : L_LINE);
     17 -	}
     18 -	return strlen(prefix) + strlen(L_LINE) + 1;
     19 -}
     20 -
     21 -static void print_prefix(const char *prefix, bool last) {
     22 -	printf("%s%s", prefix, last ? L_LAST : L_VAL);
     23 -}
     24 -
     25 -static void print_range(const struct mrsh_range *range) {
     26 -	printf("[%d:%d → %d:%d]", range->begin.line, range->begin.column,
     27 -		range->end.line, range->end.column);
     28 -}
     29 -
     30 -static const char *word_parameter_op_str(enum mrsh_word_parameter_op op) {
     31 -	switch (op) {
     32 -	case MRSH_PARAM_NONE:
     33 -		return NULL;
     34 -	case MRSH_PARAM_MINUS:
     35 -		return "-";
     36 -	case MRSH_PARAM_EQUAL:
     37 -		return "=";
     38 -	case MRSH_PARAM_QMARK:
     39 -		return "?";
     40 -	case MRSH_PARAM_PLUS:
     41 -		return "+";
     42 -	case MRSH_PARAM_LEADING_HASH:
     43 -		return "# (leading)";
     44 -	case MRSH_PARAM_PERCENT:
     45 -		return "%";
     46 -	case MRSH_PARAM_DPERCENT:
     47 -		return "%%";
     48 -	case MRSH_PARAM_HASH:
     49 -		return "#";
     50 -	case MRSH_PARAM_DHASH:
     51 -		return "##";
     52 -	}
     53 -	abort();
     54 -}
     55 -
     56 -static void print_program(struct mrsh_program *prog, const char *prefix);
     57 -
     58 -static void print_word(struct mrsh_word *word, const char *prefix) {
     59 -	char sub_prefix[make_sub_prefix(prefix, true, NULL)];
     60 -
     61 -	switch (word->type) {
     62 -	case MRSH_WORD_STRING:;
     63 -		struct mrsh_word_string *ws = mrsh_word_get_string(word);
     64 -		printf("word_string%s ", ws->single_quoted ? " (quoted)" : "");
     65 -		print_range(&ws->range);
     66 -		printf(" %s\n", ws->str);
     67 -		break;
     68 -	case MRSH_WORD_PARAMETER:;
     69 -		struct mrsh_word_parameter *wp = mrsh_word_get_parameter(word);
     70 -		printf("word_parameter\n");
     71 -
     72 -		print_prefix(prefix, wp->op == MRSH_PARAM_NONE && wp->arg == NULL);
     73 -		printf("name %s\n", wp->name);
     74 -
     75 -		if (wp->op != MRSH_PARAM_NONE) {
     76 -			print_prefix(prefix, wp->arg == NULL);
     77 -			printf("op %s%s\n",
     78 -				wp->colon ? ":" : "", word_parameter_op_str(wp->op));
     79 -		}
     80 -
     81 -		if (wp->arg != NULL) {
     82 -			make_sub_prefix(prefix, true, sub_prefix);
     83 -
     84 -			print_prefix(prefix, true);
     85 -			printf("arg ─ ");
     86 -			print_word(wp->arg, sub_prefix);
     87 -		}
     88 -		break;
     89 -	case MRSH_WORD_COMMAND:;
     90 -		struct mrsh_word_command *wc = mrsh_word_get_command(word);
     91 -		printf("word_command%s ─ ", wc->back_quoted ? " (quoted)" : "");
     92 -		print_program(wc->program, prefix);
     93 -		break;
     94 -	case MRSH_WORD_ARITHMETIC:;
     95 -		struct mrsh_word_arithmetic *wa = mrsh_word_get_arithmetic(word);
     96 -		printf("word_arithmetic ─ ");
     97 -		print_word(wa->body, prefix);
     98 -		break;
     99 -	case MRSH_WORD_LIST:;
    100 -		struct mrsh_word_list *wl = mrsh_word_get_list(word);
    101 -		printf("word_list%s\n", wl->double_quoted ? " (quoted)" : "");
    102 -
    103 -		for (size_t i = 0; i < wl->children.len; ++i) {
    104 -			struct mrsh_word *child = wl->children.data[i];
    105 -			bool last = i == wl->children.len - 1;
    106 -
    107 -			make_sub_prefix(prefix, last, sub_prefix);
    108 -
    109 -			print_prefix(prefix, last);
    110 -			print_word(child, sub_prefix);
    111 -		}
    112 -		break;
    113 -	}
    114 -}

    116 -static const char *io_redirect_op_str(enum mrsh_io_redirect_op op) {
    117 -	switch (op) {
    118 -	case MRSH_IO_LESS:
    119 -		return "<";
    120 -	case MRSH_IO_GREAT:
    121 -		return ">";
    122 -	case MRSH_IO_CLOBBER:
    123 -		return ">|";
    124 -	case MRSH_IO_DGREAT:
    125 -		return ">>";
    126 -	case MRSH_IO_LESSAND:
    127 -		return "<&";
    128 -	case MRSH_IO_GREATAND:
    129 -		return ">&";
    130 -	case MRSH_IO_LESSGREAT:
    131 -		return "<>";
    132 -	case MRSH_IO_DLESS:
    133 -		return "<<";
    134 -	case MRSH_IO_DLESSDASH:
    135 -		return "<<-";
    136 -	}
    137 -	abort();
    138 -}

    142 -static void print_io_redirect(struct mrsh_io_redirect *redir,

    146 -	print_prefix(prefix, false);

    149 -	print_prefix(prefix, false);

    360 -		bool last = i == items->len - 1;
    361 -
    362 -		char sub_prefix[make_sub_prefix(prefix, last, NULL)];
    363 -		make_sub_prefix(prefix, last, sub_prefix);
    364 -
    365 -		print_prefix(prefix, last);
    366 -		print_case_item(item, sub_prefix);
    367 -	}
    368 -}
    369 -
    370 -static void print_case_clause(struct mrsh_case_clause *cc, const char *prefix) {
    371 -	printf("case_clause\n");
    372 -
    373 -	char sub_prefix[make_sub_prefix(prefix, false, NULL)];
    374 -	make_sub_prefix(prefix, false, sub_prefix);
    375 -
    376 -	print_prefix(prefix, false);
    377 -	printf("word ─ ");
    378 -	print_word(cc->word, sub_prefix);
    379 -
    380 -	make_sub_prefix(prefix, true, sub_prefix);
    381 -
    382 -	print_prefix(prefix, true);
    383 -	printf("items\n");
    384 -	print_case_item_array(&cc->items, sub_prefix);
    385 -}
    386 -
    387 -static void print_function_definition(struct mrsh_function_definition *fd,
    388 -		const char *prefix) {
    389 -	printf("function_definition %s ─ ", fd->name);
    390 -	print_command(fd->body, prefix);
    391 -	// TODO: print io_redirects
    392 -}
    393 -
    394 -static void print_command(struct mrsh_command *cmd, const char *prefix) {
    395 -	switch (cmd->type) {
    396 -	case MRSH_SIMPLE_COMMAND:;
    397 -		struct mrsh_simple_command *sc = mrsh_command_get_simple_command(cmd);
    398 -		print_simple_command(sc, prefix);
    399 -		break;
    400 -	case MRSH_BRACE_GROUP:;
    401 -		struct mrsh_brace_group *bg = mrsh_command_get_brace_group(cmd);
    402 -		printf("brace_group\n");
    403 -		print_command_list_array(&bg->body, prefix);
    404 -		break;
    405 -	case MRSH_SUBSHELL:;
    406 -		struct mrsh_subshell *s = mrsh_command_get_subshell(cmd);
    407 -		printf("subshell\n");
    408 -		print_command_list_array(&s->body, prefix);
    409 -		break;
    410 -	case MRSH_IF_CLAUSE:;
    411 -		struct mrsh_if_clause *ic = mrsh_command_get_if_clause(cmd);
    412 -		print_if_clause(ic, prefix);
    413 -		break;
    414 -	case MRSH_FOR_CLAUSE:;
    415 -		struct mrsh_for_clause *fc = mrsh_command_get_for_clause(cmd);
    416 -		print_for_clause(fc, prefix);
    417 -		break;
    418 -	case MRSH_LOOP_CLAUSE:;
    419 -		struct mrsh_loop_clause *lc = mrsh_command_get_loop_clause(cmd);
    420 -		print_loop_clause(lc, prefix);
    421 -		break;
    422 -	case MRSH_CASE_CLAUSE:;
    423 -		struct mrsh_case_clause *cc = mrsh_command_get_case_clause(cmd);
    424 -		print_case_clause(cc, prefix);
    425 -		break;
    426 -	case MRSH_FUNCTION_DEFINITION:;
    427 -		struct mrsh_function_definition *fd =
    428 -			mrsh_command_get_function_definition(cmd);
    429 -		print_function_definition(fd, prefix);
    430 -		break;
    431 -	}
    432 -}
    433 -
    434 -static void print_pipeline(struct mrsh_pipeline *pl, const char *prefix) {
    435 -	printf("pipeline%s\n", pl->bang ? " !" : "");
    436 -
    437 -	for (size_t i = 0; i < pl->commands.len; ++i) {
    438 -		struct mrsh_command *cmd = pl->commands.data[i];
    439 -		bool last = i == pl->commands.len - 1;
    440 -
    441 -		char sub_prefix[make_sub_prefix(prefix, last, NULL)];
    442 -		make_sub_prefix(prefix, last, sub_prefix);
    443 -
    444 -		print_prefix(prefix, last);
    445 -		print_command(cmd, sub_prefix);
    446 -	}
    447 -}
    448 -
    449 -static const char *binop_type_str(enum mrsh_binop_type type) {
    450 -	switch (type) {
    451 -	case MRSH_BINOP_AND:
    452 -		return "&&";
    453 -	case MRSH_BINOP_OR:
    454 -		return "||";
    455 -	}
    456 -	return NULL;
    457 -}
    458 -
    459 -static void print_and_or_list(struct mrsh_and_or_list *and_or_list, const char *prefix);
    460 -
    461 -static void print_binop(struct mrsh_binop *binop, const char *prefix) {
    462 -	printf("binop %s\n", binop_type_str(binop->type));
    463 -
    464 -	char sub_prefix[make_sub_prefix(prefix, false, NULL)];
    465 -
    466 -	make_sub_prefix(prefix, false, sub_prefix);
    467 -	print_prefix(prefix, false);
    468 -	print_and_or_list(binop->left, sub_prefix);
    469 -
    470 -	make_sub_prefix(prefix, true, sub_prefix);
    471 -	print_prefix(prefix, true);
    472 -	print_and_or_list(binop->right, sub_prefix);
    473 -}
    474 -
    475 -static void print_and_or_list(struct mrsh_and_or_list *and_or_list, const char *prefix) {
    476 -	switch (and_or_list->type) {
    477 -	case MRSH_AND_OR_LIST_PIPELINE:;
    478 -		struct mrsh_pipeline *pl = mrsh_and_or_list_get_pipeline(and_or_list);
    479 -		print_pipeline(pl, prefix);
    480 -		break;
    481 -	case MRSH_AND_OR_LIST_BINOP:;
    482 -		struct mrsh_binop *binop = mrsh_and_or_list_get_binop(and_or_list);
    483 -		print_binop(binop, prefix);
    484 -		break;
    485 -	}
    486 -}
    487 -
    488 -static void print_command_list(struct mrsh_command_list *list,
    489 -		const char *prefix) {
    490 -	printf("command_list%s ─ ", list->ampersand ? " &" : "");
    491 -
    492 -	print_and_or_list(list->and_or_list, prefix);
    493 -}
    494 -
    495 -static void print_program(struct mrsh_program *prog, const char *prefix) {
    496 -	printf("program\n");
    497 -
    498 -	print_command_list_array(&prog->body, prefix);
    499 -}
    500 -
    501 -void mrsh_program_print(struct mrsh_program *prog) {
    502 -	print_program(prog, "");
    503 -}

    12 -
    13 -Notes:
    14 -- mrsh implements a hand-written recursive descent parser for POSIX shell.
    15 -- These references are for architecture and AST shape only; we are not copying mrsh logic directly.

  I think the formal parsing spec will be the major step for us that it has been for others.

  We are looking at how to get some ASDL/AST/LST that we can use as the foundation for our parsing and lexing.

  (Also if we go for MIT/BSD/Apache license maybe we can peel at dash ash and toysh, so put that in the vision and strategy and plan)

• Explored

• Summarizing key recommendations for a strong parser foundation: adopt POSIX grammar, create a state-machine prelexer that supports parser-driven
  tokenization with lookahead and context awareness, implement decomposition of words into parts, maintain ASTs with minimal evaluation, and design lexical
  states for quotes and substitutions. Next, focus on a recursive descent PoC with a re-entrant prelexer and logging of sources as noted.

  Confirming research logging and summarizing parser foundation

  I've logged the research PDF under the original URL and moved it to the research folder as requested. I'll provide a concise summary emphasizing adopting
  Morbig's architecture, a state-machine prelexer, context-dependent lexing, incremental parsing with parser feedback, and building an LST from word parts
  aligned with Oil, while noting dynamic runtime parsing limitations; then I'll propose next steps and ask about creating a design doc or starting
  implementation.

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Moved the PDF to research/parser/sources/sciencedirect_shell_parsing.pdf and logged it under the original URL in research/parser/sources/README.md.

  Here are my thoughts after reading all four sources (Morbig paper + both slide decks + Lobsters thread). This is what I think will put our parser/lexer on a
  firm foundation.

  What the sources imply for our parser/lexer

  1. Tokenization must be parsing‑dependent. Both slide decks and the Morbig paper emphasize that POSIX shell lexing cannot be expressed as a simple
     longest‑match regex lexer. Newlines, reserved words, and token boundaries depend on parser state and nesting (quotes, substitutions, here‑docs). We
     should make lexing an interface that accepts parser context and returns the next token accordingly.
  2. We need a “prelexer → word parser → grammar parser” structure. Morbig’s architecture separates “token recognition” (operators vs words) from “word
     parsing” (word components, quotes, expansions). This maps well to our plan: the prelexer identifies WORD/OPERATOR/NEWLINE/etc based on shell state; the
     word parser decomposes a WORD into parts for AST/LST.
  3. Speculative parsing and longest‑prefix decisions are essential. The Morbig paper lists speculative parsing, parser state introspection, context‑dependent
     lexing, and longest‑prefix parsing as requirements. This is a warning that naive recursive descent will hit ambiguities unless we explicitly implement
     backtracking or lookahead to disambiguate constructs (e.g., for function definitions vs assignments vs command words).
  4. Reserved words are contextual, not lexical. if, for, in, do, then, etc. only count as reserved words in certain parser states. This should be part of the
     parser context passed into the lexer.
  5. Here‑doc capture must be tied to parsing. Here‑doc bodies are not lexed like normal input; they depend on parsing the redirection operator and then
     capturing lines with special rules (quoting of delimiter, <<- stripping tabs). This has to be wired into the parser/lexer handshake, not as a standalone
     “read from stdin” step.
  6. Alias/eval are inherently dynamic and can make parsing undecidable. Morbig treats alias in restricted ways and excludes full eval. For mctash, since we
     are an interpreter, we can allow dynamic constructs, but for a stable AST/LST we need to accept that full static parsing isn’t always possible. We should
     define which constructs are parsed statically and which require runtime parsing or are disallowed in the static subset.

  Concrete foundation plan (for mctash)

  1. Adopt a parser‑driven lexer interface.
     Implement a TokenReader that accepts a context object from the parser and returns tokens. Context should include:
      - Whether reserved words are allowed at this position.
      - Whether newline is significant or ignorable.
      - Current quote/substitution nesting.
      - Pending here‑docs and their exact capture rules.
  2. Split WORD parsing into a dedicated word parser.
     Prelexer returns a single WORD token as raw text plus minimal metadata (quote state, escapes). A word parser converts this into a structured word list
     (e.g., literal, parameter expansion, command substitution, arithmetic expansion, quoted segments). This is where we map to the ASDL word parts.
  3. Implement a concrete syntax tree (LST/CST) first.
     The Morbig paper stresses building a complete syntax tree without evaluation. That lines up with our use of Oil’s ASDL/LST. For early milestones, we
     build LST nodes and only then convert to a simplified AST for execution.
  4. Plan for speculative parse or controlled backtracking.
     Add limited lookahead/backtracking for ambiguous constructs. The Morbig paper shows this is required for POSIX shell parsing. In Python, we can do a
     small “checkpoint and rewind” on the token reader for ambiguous productions.
  5. Model lexing state explicitly, not implicitly.
     Maintain a lexing state machine (quote mode, substitution depth, here‑doc mode, comment mode, etc.) rather than ad‑hoc string scans.

  How this interacts with the ASDL/LST

  - The ASDL/LST from Oil becomes the target “shape” for our parsed output.
  - The prelexer and word parser should produce structures that map directly onto these ASDL types.
  - This will reduce the risk of divergence from an established, audited grammar, and sets us up for later optimization.

  If you want, I can convert this into a short design note in docs/ and then start refactoring our current lexer/parser accordingly. The next concrete steps
  I’d suggest are:

  1. Add a small token_reader layer with parser context (reserved words, newline significance, here‑doc queue).
  2. Implement a minimal “word parser” that converts a WORD token into word parts (even if it’s only literal + basic $var at first).
  3. Update the parser to construct LST nodes (in parallel with our current AST) so we can start aligning to the ASDL.

  Tell me if you want me to proceed with that refactor or just capture the design first.


› Don't we already have an ASDL from osh?


• Yes. We already have the Oil/OSH ASDL in research/parser/osh_syntax/:

  - research/parser/osh_syntax/frontend.syntax.asdl
  - research/parser/osh_syntax/frontend.types.asdl
  - research/parser/osh_syntax/core.runtime.asdl
  - research/parser/osh_syntax/core.value.asdl

  There’s also a provenance/URL log in research/parser/osh_syntax/README.md and the Apache 2.0 license in research/parser/osh_syntax/LICENSE.txt.

  If you want, I can start wiring our parser/lexer outputs to those ASDL definitions next.

  our current lexer/parser accordingly. Then, as you say:

• I'm preparing to add an lst_nodes module alongside ast_nodes and adapt the parser to generate lst nodes in parallel, introducing a token_reader layer for
  this purpose. Next, I'll review the lexer and parser code.

• Explored
  └ Read lexer.py

• I'm preparing to create a token_reader layer, likely as a new module or class, and adding minimal LST node dataclasses in a new file to support parsing.
  Next, I'll review the parser's integration with these to build AST and LST structures.

• Explored
  └ Read parser.py

• I'm deciding to refactor by introducing a token reader and a minimal word parser, keeping AST nodes while adding LST nodes, and updating the runtime to
  accept Word objects instead of just strings.

• Explored
  └ Read ast_nodes.py

• Planning to build LST nodes alongside the current AST during parsing by extending the Script dataclass with an optional LST field, preserving existing APIs
  and runtime compatibility. Next is to create LST classes and update the parser to construct and attach LST nodes in parallel without breaking the existing
  AST structure.

  Planning token reader and LST node architecture

  Deciding to implement a TokenReader class with context-aware tokenization supporting reserved words, newline handling, and heredoc queuing, while minimally
  refactoring lexer logic. Simultaneously, outlining a parallel LST structure with dataclasses mirroring AST nodes, integrating LST construction into parser
  methods by returning AST and LST tuples to build both hierarchies in tandem.

• Explored
  └ Search parse_next in .

• I'm deciding to keep parse_next returning only the AST while adding a last_lst property for parallel LST construction. I'll update parsing functions to
  return (AST, LST) tuples internally and refactor the parser to accept a new TokenReader module instead of raw tokens for better context handling.

  Checking lexer.tokenize usage to decide parse input

• Explored
  └ Search tokenize\( in mctash

• Planning to add a new token_reader layer that provides tokens on demand with contextual info like reserved words and newline handling, shifting the parser
  to use it directly; tokenize can remain for simple full-token lists.

  Refining token reader to handle context and buffering

  Planning TokenReader to handle newline emission based on context and to queue heredoc tokens properly, with parser managing context consistently to avoid
  token misclassification; will implement parser peek/advance buffering tied to context to keep lexing correct.

• Explored
  └ Read runtime.py

• Noted that runtime's eval uses tokenize and requires updating to use the Parser with source; will check other usages next.

• Explored
  └ Read runtime.py

• I'll modify the new parser to initialize with a source and update main.py accordingly to parse source input. Next, I'll inspect main.py to guide these
  changes.

• Explored
  └ Read main.py

• I'm outlining a multi-file refactor to replace tokenize with a new parser API using TokenReader and LexContext, adding an optional LstScript field in the
  AST, and creating design notes and research summaries in docs and research/parser directories. Next, I'll implement the outlined file changes using
  apply_patch.

  Detailing token reader and parser design

  I'm specifying TokenReader's handling of multiple pending heredocs as a queue and its newline/token flow with LexContext controlling reserved words and
  newline allowance. Also defining word parsing into structured parts and a new Parser class with buffered tokens using contexts. Next, I'll implement these
  parser components along with updating docs and research notes per the design.

• Added src/mctash/word_parser.py (+56 -0)
     1 +from __future__ import annotations
     2 +
     3 +from .lst_nodes import LstLiteralPart, LstParamPart, LstWord, LstWordPart
     4 +
     5 +
     6 +def parse_word(text: str) -> LstWord:
     7 +    parts: list[LstWordPart] = []
     8 +    buf: list[str] = []
     9 +    i = 0
    10 +    while i < len(text):
    11 +        ch = text[i]
    12 +        if ch == "$":
    13 +            name, consumed = _parse_param(text, i)
    14 +            if name is not None:
    15 +                if buf:
    16 +                    parts.append(LstLiteralPart("".join(buf)))
    17 +                    buf = []
    18 +                parts.append(LstParamPart(name=name))
    19 +                i += consumed
    20 +                continue
    21 +        buf.append(ch)
    22 +        i += 1
    23 +    if buf:
    24 +        parts.append(LstLiteralPart("".join(buf)))
    25 +    if not parts:
    26 +        parts.append(LstLiteralPart(""))
    27 +    return LstWord(parts=parts)
    28 +
    29 +
    30 +def _parse_param(text: str, start: int) -> tuple[str | None, int]:
    31 +    if start + 1 >= len(text):
    32 +        return None, 1
    33 +    if text[start + 1] == "{":
    34 +        end = text.find("}", start + 2)
    35 +        if end == -1:
    36 +            return None, 1
    37 +        name = text[start + 2 : end]
    38 +        if not _valid_name(name):
    39 +            return None, 1
    40 +        return name, end - start + 1
    41 +    i = start + 1
    42 +    name_chars: list[str] = []
    43 +    while i < len(text) and (text[i].isalnum() or text[i] == "_"):
    44 +        name_chars.append(text[i])
    45 +        i += 1
    46 +    if not name_chars:
    47 +        return None, 1
    48 +    return "".join(name_chars), i - start
    49 +
    50 +
    51 +def _valid_name(name: str) -> bool:
    52 +    if not name:
    53 +        return False
    54 +    if not (name[0].isalpha() or name[0] == "_"):
    55 +        return False
    56 +    return all(ch.isalnum() or ch == "_" for ch in name)

     37 +@dataclass(frozen=True)
     38 +class LexContext:
     39 +    reserved_words: set[str]
     40 +    allow_reserved: bool = True
     41 +    allow_newline: bool = True
     42 +
     43 +
     44 +class TokenReader:
     45 +    def __init__(self, source: str):
     46 +        self.source = source
     47 +        self.i = 0
     48 +        self.line = 1
     49 +        self.col = 1
     50 +        self._queue: list[Token] = []
     51 +        self._pending_heredocs: list[tuple[str, bool, bool]] = []
     52
     38 -def tokenize(source: str) -> Iterator[Token]:
     39 -    i = 0
     40 -    line = 1
     41 -    col = 1
     42 -    pending_heredoc: tuple[str, bool, bool] | None = None  # (delimiter, strip_tabs, quoted)
     53 +    def next(self, ctx: LexContext) -> Optional[Token]:
     54 +        if self._queue:
     55 +            return self._queue.pop(0)
     56 +        while self.i < len(self.source):
     57 +            ch = self._peek()
     58 +            if ch in " \t\r":
     59 +                self._advance()
     60 +                continue
     61 +            if ch == "#":
     62 +                while self.i < len(self.source) and self._peek() != "\n":
     63 +                    self._advance()
     64 +                continue
     65 +            if ch == "\\" and self._peek(1) == "\n":
     66 +                self._advance(2)
     67 +                continue
     68 +            if ch == "\n":
     69 +                newline_token = Token("OP", "\n", self.line, self.col, self.i)
     70 +                self._advance()
     71 +                if self._pending_heredocs:
     72 +                    self._capture_heredocs()
     73 +                if ctx.allow_newline:
     74 +                    return newline_token
     75 +                if self._queue:
     76 +                    return self._queue.pop(0)
     77 +                continue
     78 +
     79 +            three = ch + self._peek(1) + self._peek(2)
     80 +            if three in OPERATORS:
     81 +                tok = Token("OP", three, self.line, self.col, self.i)
     82 +                self._advance(3)
     83 +                if three == "<<-":
     84 +                    self._pending_heredocs.append(("<PENDING>", True, False))
     85 +                return tok
     86 +            two = ch + self._peek(1)
     87 +            if two in OPERATORS:
     88 +                tok = Token("OP", two, self.line, self.col, self.i)
     89 +                self._advance(2)
     90 +                if two == "<<":
     91 +                    self._pending_heredocs.append(("<PENDING>", False, False))
     92 +                return tok
     93 +            if ch in OPERATORS:
     94 +                tok = Token("OP", ch, self.line, self.col, self.i)
     95 +                self._advance()
     96 +                return tok
     97 +
     98 +            start_line, start_col, start_index = self.line, self.col, self.i
     99 +            buf: List[str] = []
    100 +            while self.i < len(self.source):
    101 +                ch = self._peek()
    102 +                if ch in " \t\r\n":
    103 +                    break
    104 +                two = ch + self._peek(1)
    105 +                if two in OPERATORS or ch in OPERATORS:
    106 +                    break
    107 +                if ch == "'":
    108 +                    buf.append("'")
    109 +                    self._advance()
    110 +                    while self.i < len(self.source) and self._peek() != "'":
    111 +                        buf.append(self._peek())
    112 +                        self._advance()
    113 +                    if self._peek() == "'":
    114 +                        buf.append("'")
    115 +                        self._advance()
    116 +                    continue
    117 +                if ch == '"':
    118 +                    buf.append('"')
    119 +                    self._advance()
    120 +                    while self.i < len(self.source) and self._peek() != '"':
    121 +                        if self._peek() == "\\" and self._peek(1) in ['"', "\\", "$", "`"]:
    122 +                            buf.append("\\")
    123 +                            self._advance()
    124 +                            buf.append(self._peek())
    125 +                            self._advance()
    126 +                            continue
    127 +                        buf.append(self._peek())
    128 +                        self._advance()
    129 +                    if self._peek() == '"':
    130 +                        buf.append('"')
    131 +                        self._advance()
    132 +                    continue
    133 +                if ch == "$" and self._peek(1) == "(":
    134 +                    buf.append("$(")
    135 +                    self._advance(2)
    136 +                    depth = 1
    137 +                    while self.i < len(self.source) and depth > 0:
    138 +                        if self._peek() == "(":
    139 +                            depth += 1
    140 +                            buf.append(self._peek())
    141 +                            self._advance()
    142 +                            continue
    143 +                        if self._peek() == ")":
    144 +                            depth -= 1
    145 +                            buf.append(")")
    146 +                            self._advance()
    147 +                            if depth == 0:

    198 +def tokenize(source: str) -> Iterator[Token]:
    199 +    ctx = LexContext(reserved_words=set())
    200 +    reader = TokenReader(source)
    201 +    while True:
    202 +        tok = reader.next(ctx)
    203 +        if tok is None:
    204 +            break
    205 +        yield tok
    206

• Planning a full parser.py rewrite using token_reader, lst_nodes, and word_parser for clearer structure; each parse method returns an AST and LST node tuple,
  with word parsing unified via word_parser.parse_word and updated assignment parsing to produce LST values. Next, I'll apply a precise patch or full rewrite
  reflecting this new design.

  Detailing parser updates and helper additions

  Refining parsing of assignments, redirects, and case patterns to produce LST words alongside AST strings, adding _peek_n helper to support lookahead via
  buffered tokens with context options, and updating token kind checks for reserved words and operators; planning a full parser.py replacement via patch for
  clarity and consistency.

    373 -                elif_body = self.parse_compound_list({"else", "elif", "fi"})
    374 -                elifs.append((elif_cond, elif_body))
    375 -                continue
    376 -            break
    377 -        tok = self._peek()
    378 -        if tok and tok.kind == "WORD" and tok.value == "else":
    379 -            self._advance()
    380 -            else_body = self.parse_compound_list({"fi"})
    381 -        end_tok = self._advance()
    382 -        if end_tok is None or end_tok.kind != "WORD" or end_tok.value != "fi":
    383 -            raise ParseError(f"expected fi at {self._where(end_tok)}")
    384 -        return IfCommand(cond=cond, then_body=then_body, elifs=elifs, else_body=else_body)
    385 -
    386 -    def parse_while(self) -> WhileCommand:
    387 -        tok = self._advance()
    388 -        if tok is None or tok.kind != "WORD" or tok.value not in ["while", "until"]:
    389 -            raise ParseError(f"expected while/until at {self._where(tok)}")
    390 -        until = tok.value == "until"
    391 -        cond = self.parse_compound_list({"do"})
    392 -        do_tok = self._advance()
    393 -        if do_tok is None or do_tok.kind != "WORD" or do_tok.value != "do":
    394 -            raise ParseError(f"expected do at {self._where(do_tok)}")
    395 -        body = self.parse_compound_list({"done"})
    396 -        done_tok = self._advance()
    397 -        if done_tok is None or done_tok.kind != "WORD" or done_tok.value != "done":
    398 -            raise ParseError(f"expected done at {self._where(done_tok)}")
    399 -        return WhileCommand(cond=cond, body=body, until=until)
    400 -
    401 -    def _looks_like_function_def(self) -> bool:
    402 -        tok = self._peek()
    403 -        if tok is None or tok.kind != "WORD":
    404 -            return False
    405 -        if self.pos + 2 >= len(self.tokens):
    406 -            return False
    407 -        tok1 = self.tokens[self.pos + 1]
    408 -        tok2 = self.tokens[self.pos + 2]

    410 -            return True
    411 -        return False

    413 -
    414 -def parse(tokens: List[Token]) -> Script:

• Added src/mctash/parser.py (+534 -0)
      1 +from __future__ import annotations
      2 +
      3 +from typing import List, Optional
      4 +
      5 +from .ast_nodes import (
      6 +    AndOr,
      7 +    Assignment,
      8 +    CaseCommand,
      9 +    CaseItem,
     10 +    Command,
     11 +    ForCommand,
     12 +    FunctionDef,
     13 +    GroupCommand,
     14 +    IfCommand,
     15 +    ListNode,
     16 +    Pipeline,
     17 +    Redirect,
     18 +    Script,
     19 +    SimpleCommand,
     20 +    SubshellCommand,
     21 +    WhileCommand,
     22 +    Word,
     23 +)
     24 +from .lexer import LexContext, Token, TokenReader
     25 +from .lst_nodes import (
     26 +    LstAndOr,
     27 +    LstAssignment,
     28 +    LstCaseCommand,
     29 +    LstCaseItem,
     30 +    LstCommand,
     31 +    LstForCommand,
     32 +    LstFunctionDef,
     33 +    LstGroupCommand,
     34 +    LstIfCommand,
     35 +    LstListNode,
     36 +    LstPipeline,
     37 +    LstRedirect,
     38 +    LstScript,
     39 +    LstSimpleCommand,
     40 +    LstSubshellCommand,
     41 +    LstWhileCommand,
     42 +)
     43 +from .word_parser import parse_word
     44 +
     45 +
     46 +class ParseError(Exception):
     47 +    pass
     48 +
     49 +
     50 +RESERVED_WORDS = {
     51 +    "if",
     52 +    "then",
     53 +    "else",
     54 +    "elif",
     55 +    "fi",
     56 +    "for",
     57 +    "in",
     58 +    "do",
     59 +    "done",
     60 +    "case",
     61 +    "esac",
     62 +    "while",
     63 +    "until",
     64 +    "function",
     65 +}
     66 +
     67 +
     68 +class Parser:
     69 +    def __init__(self, source: str):
     70 +        self.reader = TokenReader(source)
     71 +        self.buffer: list[Token] = []
     72 +        self.ctx = LexContext(reserved_words=RESERVED_WORDS, allow_reserved=True, allow_newline=True)
     73 +        self.last_lst: Optional[LstAndOr] = None
     74 +
     75 +    def _peek(self) -> Optional[Token]:
     76 +        if not self.buffer:
     77 +            tok = self.reader.next(self.ctx)
     78 +            if tok is not None:
     79 +                self.buffer.append(tok)
     80 +        return self.buffer[0] if self.buffer else None
     81 +
     82 +    def _peek_n(self, n: int) -> Optional[Token]:
     83 +        while len(self.buffer) <= n:
     84 +            tok = self.reader.next(self.ctx)
     85 +            if tok is None:
     86 +                break
     87 +            self.buffer.append(tok)
     88 +        if n < len(self.buffer):
     89 +            return self.buffer[n]
     90 +        return None
     91 +
     92 +    def _advance(self) -> Optional[Token]:
     93 +        tok = self._peek()
     94 +        if tok is not None:
     95 +            self.buffer.pop(0)
     96 +        return tok
     97 +
     98 +    def _expect_op(self, op: str) -> Token:
     99 +        tok = self._peek()
    100 +        if tok is None or tok.kind != "OP" or tok.value != op:
    101 +            raise ParseError(f"expected {op!r} at {self._where(tok)}")
    102 +        self._advance()
    103 +        return tok
    104 +
    105 +    def _where(self, tok: Optional[Token]) -> str:
    106 +        if tok is None:
    107 +            return "eof"
    108 +        return f"{tok.line}:{tok.col}"
    109 +
    110 +    def _is_word(self, tok: Optional[Token]) -> bool:
    111 +        return tok is not None and tok.kind in ["WORD", "RESERVED"]
    112 +
    113 +    def parse_script(self) -> Script:
    114 +        body, lst_body = self.parse_list()
    115 +        lst_script = LstScript(body=lst_body)
    116 +        return Script(body=body, lst=lst_script)
    117 +
    118 +    def parse_next(self) -> Optional[AndOr]:
    119 +        while True:
    120 +            tok = self._peek()
    121 +            if tok is None:
    122 +                return None
    123 +            if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in ["\n", ";"]:
    124 +                self._advance()
    125 +                continue
    126 +            break
    127 +        node, lst_node = self.parse_and_or()
    128 +        self.last_lst = lst_node
    129 +        tok = self._peek()
    130 +        if tok and tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in ["\n", ";"]:
    131 +            self._advance()
    132 +        return node
    133 +
    134 +    def parse_list(self) -> tuple[ListNode, LstListNode]:
    135 +        items: List[AndOr] = []
    136 +        lst_items: List[LstAndOr] = []
    137 +        while True:
    138 +            if self._peek() is None:
    139 +                break
    140 +            if self._peek().kind == "OP" and self._peek().value == "\n":
    141 +                self._advance()
    142 +                continue
    143 +            item, lst_item = self.parse_and_or()
    144 +            items.append(item)
    145 +            lst_items.append(lst_item)
    146 +            tok = self._peek()
    147 +            if tok is None:
    148 +                break
    149 +            if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in [";", "\n"]:
    150 +                self._advance()
    151 +                continue
    152 +        return ListNode(items=items), LstListNode(items=lst_items)
    153 +
    154 +    def parse_compound_list(self, stop_words: set[str]) -> tuple[ListNode, LstListNode]:
    155 +        items: List[AndOr] = []
    156 +        lst_items: List[LstAndOr] = []
    157 +        while True:
    158 +            tok = self._peek()
    159 +            if tok is None:
    160 +                break
    161 +            if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value in stop_words:
    162 +                break
    163 +            if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in stop_words:
    164 +                break
    165 +            if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in ["\n", ";"]:
    166 +                self._advance()
    167 +                continue
    168 +            item, lst_item = self.parse_and_or()
    169 +            items.append(item)
    170 +            lst_items.append(lst_item)
    171 +            tok = self._peek()
    172 +            if tok is None:
    173 +                break
    174 +            if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in [";", "\n"]:
    175 +                self._advance()
    176 +                continue
    177 +        return ListNode(items=items), LstListNode(items=lst_items)
    178 +
    179 +    def parse_and_or(self) -> tuple[AndOr, LstAndOr]:
    180 +        pipeline, lst_pipeline = self.parse_pipeline()
    181 +        pipelines: List[Pipeline] = [pipeline]
    182 +        lst_pipelines: List[LstPipeline] = [lst_pipeline]
    183 +        operators: List[str] = []
    184 +        while True:
    185 +            tok = self._peek()
    186 +            if tok and tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in ["&&", "||"]:
    187 +                operators.append(tok.value)
    188 +                self._advance()
    189 +                pipeline, lst_pipeline = self.parse_pipeline()
    190 +                pipelines.append(pipeline)
    191 +                lst_pipelines.append(lst_pipeline)
    192 +                continue
    193 +            break
    194 +        return AndOr(pipelines=pipelines, operators=operators), LstAndOr(pipelines=lst_pipelines, operators=operators)
    195 +
    196 +    def parse_pipeline(self) -> tuple[Pipeline, LstPipeline]:
    197 +        negate = False
    198 +        tok = self._peek()
    199 +        if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "!":
    200 +            self._advance()
    201 +            negate = True
    202 +        command, lst_command = self.parse_command()
    203 +        commands: List[Command] = [command]
    204 +        lst_commands: List[LstCommand] = [lst_command]
    205 +        while True:
    206 +            tok = self._peek()
    207 +            if tok and tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value == "|":
    208 +                self._advance()
    209 +                command, lst_command = self.parse_command()
    210 +                commands.append(command)
    211 +                lst_commands.append(lst_command)
    212 +                continue
    213 +            break
    214 +        return Pipeline(commands=commands, negate=negate), LstPipeline(commands=lst_commands, negate=negate)
    215 +
    216 +    def parse_command(self) -> tuple[Command, LstCommand]:
    217 +        tok = self._peek()
    218 +        if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "if":
    219 +            return self.parse_if()
    220 +        if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value in ["while", "until"]:
    221 +            return self.parse_while()
    222 +        if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "for":
    223 +            return self.parse_for()
    224 +        if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "case":
    225 +            return self.parse_case()
    226 +        if tok and tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value == "{":
    227 +            return self.parse_group()
    228 +        if tok and tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value == "(":
    229 +            return self.parse_subshell()
    230 +        if self._looks_like_function_def():
    231 +            return self.parse_function_def()
    232 +
    233 +        argv: List[Word] = []
    234 +        lst_argv: List = []
    235 +        assignments: List[Assignment] = []
    236 +        lst_assignments: List[LstAssignment] = []
    237 +        redirects: List[Redirect] = []
    238 +        lst_redirects: List[LstRedirect] = []
    239 +        while True:
    240 +            tok = self._peek()
    241 +            if tok is None:
    242 +                break
    243 +            if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value.isdigit():
    244 +                next_tok = self._peek_n(1)
    245 +                if next_tok and next_tok.kind == "OP" and next_tok.value in ["<", ">", ">>", "<<", "<<-", ">&", "<&"]:
    246 +                    fd = int(tok.value)
    247 +                    self._advance()
    248 +                    op_tok = self._advance()
    249 +                    target_tok = self._peek()
    250 +                    if target_tok is None or not self._is_word(target_tok):
    251 +                        raise ParseError(f"expected redirection target at {self._where(target_tok)}")
    252 +                    redir = self._make_redirect(op_tok.value, target_tok.value, fd)
    253 +                    lst_redir = self._make_lst_redirect(op_tok.value, target_tok.value, fd)
    254 +                    self._advance()
    255 +                    if redir.op == "<<":
    256 +                        here_tok = self._peek()
    257 +                        if here_tok and here_tok.kind == "HEREDOC":
    258 +                            self._apply_heredoc_token(redir, lst_redir, here_tok.value)
    259 +                            self._advance()
    260 +                    if redir.op == "<<-":
    261 +                        here_tok = self._peek()
    262 +                        if here_tok and here_tok.kind == "HEREDOC":
    263 +                            self._apply_heredoc_token(redir, lst_redir, here_tok.value)
    264 +                            self._advance()
    265 +                    redirects.append(redir)
    266 +                    lst_redirects.append(lst_redir)
    267 +                    continue
    268 +            if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in ["<", ">", ">>", "<<", "<<-", ">&", "<&"]:
    269 +                op = tok.value
    270 +                self._advance()
    271 +                target_tok = self._peek()
    272 +                if target_tok is None or not self._is_word(target_tok):
    273 +                    raise ParseError(f"expected redirection target at {self._where(target_tok)}")
    274 +                redir = self._make_redirect(op, target_tok.value, None)
    275 +                lst_redir = self._make_lst_redirect(op, target_tok.value, None)
    276 +                self._advance()
    277 +                if redir.op in ["<<", "<<-"]:
    278 +                    here_tok = self._peek()
    279 +                    if here_tok and here_tok.kind == "HEREDOC":
    280 +                        self._apply_heredoc_token(redir, lst_redir, here_tok.value)
    281 +                        self._advance()
    282 +                redirects.append(redir)
    283 +                lst_redirects.append(lst_redir)
    284 +                continue
    285 +            if self._is_word(tok):
    286 +                if self._is_assignment(tok.value) and not argv:
    287 +                    name, value = tok.value.split("=", 1)
    288 +                    assignments.append(Assignment(name=name, value=value))
    289 +                    lst_assignments.append(LstAssignment(name=name, value=parse_word(value)))
    290 +                else:
    291 +                    argv.append(Word(tok.value))
    292 +                    lst_argv.append(parse_word(tok.value))
    293 +                self._advance()
    294 +                continue
    295 +            break
    296 +        if not argv and not assignments and not redirects:
    297 +            raise ParseError(f"expected command at {self._where(tok)}")
    298 +        return (
    299 +            SimpleCommand(argv=argv, assignments=assignments, redirects=redirects),
    300 +            LstSimpleCommand(argv=lst_argv, assignments=lst_assignments, redirects=lst_redirects),
    301 +        )
    302 +
    303 +    def _is_assignment(self, text: str) -> bool:
    304 +        if "=" not in text:
    305 +            return False
    306 +        name, _ = text.split("=", 1)
    307 +        if not name:
    308 +            return False
    309 +        if not (name[0].isalpha() or name[0] == "_"):
    310 +            return False
    311 +        return all(ch.isalnum() or ch == "_" for ch in name)
    312 +
    313 +    def _make_redirect(self, op: str, target: str, fd: int | None) -> Redirect:
    314 +        if op == "<<-":
    315 +            return Redirect(op="<<", target=target, fd=fd, here_doc_strip_tabs=True)
    316 +        return Redirect(op=op, target=target, fd=fd)
    317 +
    318 +    def _make_lst_redirect(self, op: str, target: str, fd: int | None) -> LstRedirect:
    319 +        if op == "<<-":
    320 +            return LstRedirect(op="<<", target=parse_word(target), fd=fd, here_doc_strip_tabs=True)
    321 +        return LstRedirect(op=op, target=parse_word(target), fd=fd)
    322 +
    323 +    def _apply_heredoc_token(self, redir: Redirect, lst_redir: LstRedirect, token_value: str) -> None:
    324 +        parts = token_value.split(":", 2)
    325 +        if len(parts) == 3:
    326 +            expand_flag, strip_flag, content = parts
    327 +            redir.here_doc_expand = expand_flag == "E"
    328 +            redir.here_doc_strip_tabs = strip_flag == "T"
    329 +            redir.here_doc = content
    330 +            lst_redir.here_doc_expand = expand_flag == "E"
    331 +            lst_redir.here_doc_strip_tabs = strip_flag == "T"
    332 +            lst_redir.here_doc = content
    333 +        else:
    334 +            redir.here_doc = token_value
    335 +            lst_redir.here_doc = token_value
    336 +
    337 +    def parse_group(self) -> tuple[GroupCommand, LstGroupCommand]:
    338 +        self._expect_op("{")
    339 +        body, lst_body = self.parse_compound_list({"}"})
    340 +        self._expect_op("}")
    341 +        return GroupCommand(body=body), LstGroupCommand(body=lst_body)
    342 +
    343 +    def parse_function_def(self) -> tuple[FunctionDef, LstFunctionDef]:
    344 +        name_tok = self._advance()
    345 +        if name_tok is None or not self._is_word(name_tok):
    346 +            raise ParseError(f"expected function name at {self._where(name_tok)}")
    347 +        self._expect_op("(")
    348 +        self._expect_op(")")
    349 +        body_cmd, lst_body_cmd = self.parse_group()
    350 +        return FunctionDef(name=name_tok.value, body=body_cmd.body), LstFunctionDef(
    351 +            name=name_tok.value,
    352 +            body=lst_body_cmd.body,
    353 +        )
    354 +
    355 +    def parse_subshell(self) -> tuple[SubshellCommand, LstSubshellCommand]:
    356 +        self._expect_op("(")
    357 +        body, lst_body = self.parse_compound_list({")"})
    358 +        self._expect_op(")")
    359 +        return SubshellCommand(body=body), LstSubshellCommand(body=lst_body)
    360 +
    361 +    def parse_for(self) -> tuple[ForCommand, LstForCommand]:
    362 +        tok = self._advance()
    363 +        if tok is None or not self._is_word(tok) or tok.value != "for":
    364 +            raise ParseError(f"expected for at {self._where(tok)}")
    365 +        name_tok = self._advance()
    366 +        if name_tok is None or not self._is_word(name_tok):
    367 +            raise ParseError(f"expected name at {self._where(name_tok)}")
    368 +        items: List[Word] = []
    369 +        lst_items: List = []
    370 +        tok = self._peek()
    371 +        if tok and self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "in":
    372 +            self._advance()
    373 +            while True:
    374 +                tok = self._peek()
    375 +                if tok is None:
    376 +                    break
    377 +                if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "do":
    378 +                    break
    379 +                if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in [";", "\n"]:
    380 +                    self._advance()
    381 +                    continue
    382 +                if self._is_word(tok):
    383 +                    items.append(Word(tok.value))
    384 +                    lst_items.append(parse_word(tok.value))
    385 +                    self._advance()
    386 +                    continue
    387 +                break
    388 +        do_tok = self._advance()
    389 +        if do_tok is None or not self._is_word(do_tok) or do_tok.value != "do":
    390 +            raise ParseError(f"expected do at {self._where(do_tok)}")
    391 +        body, lst_body = self.parse_compound_list({"done"})
    392 +        done_tok = self._advance()
    393 +        if done_tok is None or not self._is_word(done_tok) or done_tok.value != "done":
    394 +            raise ParseError(f"expected done at {self._where(done_tok)}")
    395 +        return (
    396 +            ForCommand(name=name_tok.value, items=items, body=body),
    397 +            LstForCommand(name=name_tok.value, items=lst_items, body=lst_body),
    398 +        )
    399 +
    400 +    def parse_case(self) -> tuple[CaseCommand, LstCaseCommand]:
    401 +        tok = self._advance()
    402 +        if tok is None or not self._is_word(tok) or tok.value != "case":
    403 +            raise ParseError(f"expected case at {self._where(tok)}")
    404 +        word_tok = self._advance()
    405 +        if word_tok is None or not self._is_word(word_tok):
    406 +            raise ParseError(f"expected case word at {self._where(word_tok)}")
    407 +        in_tok = self._advance()
    408 +        if in_tok is None or not self._is_word(in_tok) or in_tok.value != "in":
    409 +            raise ParseError(f"expected in at {self._where(in_tok)}")
    410 +        items: List[CaseItem] = []
    411 +        lst_items: List[LstCaseItem] = []
    412 +        while True:
    413 +            tok = self._peek()
    414 +            if tok is None:
    415 +                break
    416 +            if self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "esac":
    417 +                self._advance()
    418 +                break
    419 +            if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value in ["\n", ";"]:
    420 +                self._advance()
    421 +                continue
    422 +            patterns: List[str] = []
    423 +            lst_patterns: List = []
    424 +            while True:
    425 +                tok = self._peek()
    426 +                if tok is None:
    427 +                    break
    428 +                if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value == ")":
    429 +                    self._advance()
    430 +                    break
    431 +                if self._is_word(tok):
    432 +                    part = tok.value
    433 +                    self._advance()
    434 +                    if part.endswith("|"):
    435 +                        part = part[:-1]
    436 +                        patterns.append(part)
    437 +                        lst_patterns.append(parse_word(part))
    438 +                        continue
    439 +                    patterns.append(part)
    440 +                    lst_patterns.append(parse_word(part))
    441 +                    tok2 = self._peek()
    442 +                    if tok2 and tok2.kind == "OP" and tok2.value == "|":
    443 +                        self._advance()
    444 +                        continue
    445 +                    continue
    446 +                if tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value == "|":
    447 +                    self._advance()
    448 +                    continue
    449 +                break
    450 +            body, lst_body = self.parse_compound_list({";;", "esac"})
    451 +            tok = self._peek()
    452 +            if tok and tok.kind == "OP" and tok.value == ";;":
    453 +                self._advance()
    454 +            items.append(CaseItem(patterns=patterns, body=body))
    455 +            lst_items.append(LstCaseItem(patterns=lst_patterns, body=lst_body))
    456 +        return (
    457 +            CaseCommand(value=Word(word_tok.value), items=items),
    458 +            LstCaseCommand(value=parse_word(word_tok.value), items=lst_items),
    459 +        )
    460 +
    461 +    def parse_if(self) -> tuple[IfCommand, LstIfCommand]:
    462 +        tok = self._advance()
    463 +        if tok is None or tok.value != "if":
    464 +            raise ParseError(f"expected if at {self._where(tok)}")
    465 +        cond, lst_cond = self.parse_compound_list({"then"})
    466 +        then_tok = self._advance()
    467 +        if then_tok is None or not self._is_word(then_tok) or then_tok.value != "then":
    468 +            raise ParseError(f"expected then at {self._where(then_tok)}")
    469 +        then_body, lst_then_body = self.parse_compound_list({"else", "elif", "fi"})
    470 +        elifs: List[tuple[ListNode, ListNode]] = []
    471 +        lst_elifs: List[tuple[LstListNode, LstListNode]] = []
    472 +        else_body = None
    473 +        lst_else_body = None
    474 +        while True:
    475 +            tok = self._peek()
    476 +            if tok and self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "elif":
    477 +                self._advance()
    478 +                elif_cond, lst_elif_cond = self.parse_compound_list({"then"})
    479 +                then_tok = self._advance()
    480 +                if then_tok is None or not self._is_word(then_tok) or then_tok.value != "then":
    481 +                    raise ParseError(f"expected then at {self._where(then_tok)}")
    482 +                elif_body, lst_elif_body = self.parse_compound_list({"else", "elif", "fi"})
    483 +                elifs.append((elif_cond, elif_body))
    484 +                lst_elifs.append((lst_elif_cond, lst_elif_body))
    485 +                continue
    486 +            break
    487 +        tok = self._peek()
    488 +        if tok and self._is_word(tok) and tok.value == "else":
    489 +            self._advance()
    490 +            else_body, lst_else_body = self.parse_compound_list({"fi"})
    491 +        end_tok = self._advance()
    492 +        if end_tok is None or not self._is_word(end_tok) or end_tok.value != "fi":
    493 +            raise ParseError(f"expected fi at {self._where(end_tok)}")
    494 +        return (
    495 +            IfCommand(cond=cond, then_body=then_body, elifs=elifs, else_body=else_body),
    496 +            LstIfCommand(
    497 +                cond=lst_cond,
    498 +                then_body=lst_then_body,
    499 +                elifs=lst_elifs,
    500 +                else_body=lst_else_body,
    501 +            ),
    502 +        )
    503 +
    504 +    def parse_while(self) -> tuple[WhileCommand, LstWhileCommand]:
    505 +        tok = self._advance()
    506 +        if tok is None or not self._is_word(tok) or tok.value not in ["while", "until"]:
    507 +            raise ParseError(f"expected while/until at {self._where(tok)}")
    508 +        until = tok.value == "until"
    509 +        cond, lst_cond = self.parse_compound_list({"do"})
    510 +        do_tok = self._advance()
    511 +        if do_tok is None or not self._is_word(do_tok) or do_tok.value != "do":
    512 +            raise ParseError(f"expected do at {self._where(do_tok)}")
    513 +        body, lst_body = self.parse_compound_list({"done"})
    514 +        done_tok = self._advance()
    515 +        if done_tok is None or not self._is_word(done_tok) or done_tok.value != "done":
    516 +            raise ParseError(f"expected done at {self._where(done_tok)}")
    517 +        return (
    518 +            WhileCommand(cond=cond, body=body, until=until),
    519 +            LstWhileCommand(cond=lst_cond, body=lst_body, until=until),
    520 +        )
    521 +
    522 +    def _looks_like_function_def(self) -> bool:
    523 +        tok = self._peek()
    524 +        if tok is None or not self._is_word(tok):
    525 +            return False
    526 +        tok1 = self._peek_n(1)
    527 +        tok2 = self._peek_n(2)
    528 +        if tok1 and tok1.kind == "OP" and tok1.value == "(" and tok2 and tok2.kind == "OP" and tok2.value == ")":
    529 +            return True
    530 +        return False
    531 +
    532 +
    533 +def parse(source: str) -> Script:
    534 +    return Parser(source).parse_script()

• Added research/parser/README.md (+33 -0)
     1 +# Parser Research Notes
     2 +
     3 +This directory tracks parser/lexer research relevant to mctash and summarizes
     4 +how those sources affect our design.
     5 +
     6 +## Sources
     7 +- `research/parser/sources/regis_gianas_pps2018.pdf`
     8 +- `research/parser/sources/fosdem_posix_shell_parsing.pdf`
     9 +- `research/parser/sources/sciencedirect_shell_parsing.pdf`
    10 +- `research/parser/sources/lobsters_formally_verified_shell.html`
    11 +- `research/parser/osh_syntax/` (Oil/OSH ASDL/LST files, Apache 2.0)
    12 +- `research/parser/mrsh/` (mrsh parser sources, MIT)
    13 +
    14 +## Key Findings
    15 +- POSIX shell lexing is parsing-dependent. Newlines, reserved words, and token
    16 +  boundaries depend on parser state and nesting (quotes, substitutions, here-docs).
    17 +- The usual "regex lexer then parser" pipeline is not sufficient; a prelexer
    18 +  that cooperates with the parser is needed for reliable token recognition.
    19 +- Word parsing (quote/expansion structure) is its own problem and should be
    20 +  separated from pretokenization.
    21 +- Here-doc capture must be triggered by parsing (redirection operators and
    22 +  their delimiters), not by reading stdin opportunistically.
    23 +- Some constructs are inherently dynamic (alias/eval). Static parsing needs a
    24 +  defined subset and explicit rules for what is rejected or deferred to runtime.
    25 +- Using an ASDL/LST (from Oil/OSH) gives a stable target shape for the parse
    26 +  tree and reduces ambiguity about what structures we should emit.
    27 +
    28 +## Design Implications (for mctash)
    29 +- Build a token reader that accepts parser context (reserved words, newline
    30 +  significance) and maintains a here-doc queue.
    31 +- Split word parsing into a dedicated phase that produces word parts.
    32 +- Parse into LST nodes alongside the current AST, then convert LST -> AST
    33 +  as the implementation matures.

• Added docs/parser_design.md (+44 -0)
     1 +# Parser Design Note (WIP)
     2 +
     3 +This note captures how the parser/lexer should be structured to stay aligned
     4 +with the research in `research/parser/` and to map cleanly to the Oil/OSH ASDL.
     5 +
     6 +## Goals
     7 +- A parser-driven lexer (token reader) with explicit parser context.
     8 +- A distinct word parser that builds word parts for later expansion.
     9 +- Parallel LST construction that maps to Oil/OSH ASDL/LST types.
    10 +
    11 +## Background (Why this structure)
    12 +The POSIX shell spec and the Morbig paper show that shell lexing is
    13 +parsing-dependent: newlines, token boundaries, and reserved words depend on
    14 +parsing context and nesting. A standard regex-lexer + parser pipeline is not
    15 +enough; we need a prelexer that can be guided by the parser.
    16 +
    17 +## Proposed Stages
    18 +1. **Token Reader (Prelexer)**
    19 +   - Input: raw source + parser context.
    20 +   - Output: tokens of `OP`, `WORD`, `HEREDOC`.
    21 +   - Responsibilities:
    22 +     - Newline significance (emit or ignore).
    23 +     - Reserved-word tagging (optional, context-sensitive).
    24 +     - Here-doc queueing and capture after delimiter parse.
    25 +
    26 +2. **Word Parser**
    27 +   - Input: raw `WORD` text.
    28 +   - Output: structured word parts (literal, parameter, command subst, etc.).
    29 +   - Starts minimal (literal + `$var`) and grows to quotes/expansion rules.
    30 +
    31 +3. **Grammar Parser**
    32 +   - Produces LST nodes in parallel to AST nodes.
    33 +   - LST types match Oil/OSH ASDL structure; AST can remain a simplified view.
    34 +
    35 +## Key Constraints
    36 +- Reserved words are contextual (e.g., `in`, `do`, `then`).
    37 +- Newlines can be either token delimiters or whitespace depending on state.
    38 +- Here-doc bodies are parsed as a post-tokenization step and depend on delimiter
    39 +  quoting rules (`<<`, `<<-`, quoted delimiter).
    40 +
    41 +## Next Steps
    42 +- Introduce a token reader layer that the parser drives.
    43 +- Add a minimal word parser for `$var` and `${var}`.
    44 +- Emit LST nodes alongside AST to align with Oil/OSH ASDL early.

• I confirmed runtime references and imports align with recent parser updates, no init changes needed, and new LST nodes don't affect runtime. Noted potential
  TokenReader heredoc position handling requires ensuring correct line/column advancement after capture.

  Completing parser refactor summary and preparing user response

  I finalized minimal LST word parser and integrated it for case patterns, updated related type hints, ensured assignment nodes use LstWord, and documented
  all research and design steps in relevant files. I'll summarize these changes, highlight lexer/parser refactor details, runtime Parser usage, and suggest
  optionally running smoke tests next.

─ Worked for 4m 41s ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Done. I recorded the parser research, added the design note, and refactored the lexer/parser with the three steps you listed.

  What I changed

  1. Recorded parser research
      - Added research/parser/README.md with key findings and design implications.
      - Logged the ScienceDirect PDF in research/parser/sources/README.md and moved the file to research/parser/sources/sciencedirect_shell_parsing.pdf.
  2. Design note
      - Added docs/parser_design.md describing the parser-driven lexer, word parser, and LST alignment.
  3. Refactor (token reader, word parser, LST parallel build)
      - Added token reader and parser context in src/mctash/lexer.py.
      - Added minimal word parser in src/mctash/word_parser.py.
      - Added LST node definitions in src/mctash/lst_nodes.py.
      - Refactored src/mctash/parser.py to build LST nodes alongside the AST and to use the token reader.
      - Updated runtime and main to use the new parser API (source string).

  Files

  - Research notes: research/parser/README.md
  - Design note: docs/parser_design.md
  - Token reader: src/mctash/lexer.py
  - Word parser: src/mctash/word_parser.py
  - LST nodes: src/mctash/lst_nodes.py
  - Parser refactor: src/mctash/parser.py
  - Runtime/main updates: src/mctash/runtime.py, src/mctash/main.py

  If you want, I can now:

  1. Expand the word parser to handle quotes, command substitutions, and arithmetic.
  2. Wire LST output to a formal ASDL mapping (explicit node names/fields).
  3. Add a small debug command to print LST for a script so you can inspect it quickly.

  Tell me which direction you want next.


› Draw me a diagram to explain how the parse now fits in with the AST, ASDL, LST from Osh.


• Here’s a concise diagram of how parsing now fits with the AST, ASDL, and LST from Oil/OSH:

                   +------------------------------+
                   |  Oil/OSH ASDL (reference)    |
                   |  research/parser/osh_syntax  |
                   |  - frontend.syntax.asdl      |
                   |  - frontend.types.asdl       |
                   +--------------+---------------+
                                  |
                                  |  (shape + names we target)
                                  v
  +-------------------+    +-------------------+    +----------------------+
  |  TokenReader      | -> |  Parser           | -> |  LST (parallel)       |
  |  (parser-driven)  |    |  (grammar)        |    |  src/mctash/lst_nodes |
  |  src/mctash/lexer |    |  src/mctash/parser|    |  Lst* nodes           |
  +-------------------+    +-------------------+    +----------+-----------+
           |                        |                         |
           |                        |                         |
           |                        |                         | (future: direct ASDL mapping)
           |                        |                         v
           |                        |                +----------------------+
           |                        |                |  AST (runtime view)  |
           |                        +--------------> |  src/mctash/ast_nodes|
           |                                         +----------------------+
           |
           v
  +-------------------+
  |  Word Parser      |
  |  src/mctash/word_ |
  |  parser.py        |
  +-------------------+

  How to read this

  - The ASDL from Oil/OSH is the schema we’re aligning to.
  - Our Parser now builds LST nodes (from lst_nodes.py) alongside the existing AST.
  - The AST is still the simpler runtime view used by the executor.
  - The Word Parser is a separate phase called during parsing to build structured word parts for the LST.

  If you want a more formal diagram (e.g., Mermaid) or want me to show a specific path for a sample command, say the word.


› Yes, do the 3 suggestions in order:

    2. Wire LST output to a formal ASDL mapping (explicit node names/fields).
    3. Add a small debug command to print LST for a script so you can inspect it quickly.
